Published: May 3, 2026 | Last reviewed: May 3, 2026

Pregnancy Week by Week Urdu Mein — Trimester Wise Guide Pakistan

TL;DR — Key Takeaways
  • Pregnancy 3 trimesters mein divided — har 12 weeks
  • 1st trimester: morning sickness, fatigue, fetal organs develop
  • 2nd trimester: "honeymoon phase", baby movements feel
  • 3rd trimester: rapid growth, delivery preparation
  • Regular antenatal visits zaruri — har trimester mein

Pregnancy Ki Mukammal Journey

Pregnancy ka pura safar 40 weeks (lagbhag 9 mahine) ka hota hai, jo 3 trimesters mein divide hota hai. Har trimester mein body aur baby ka unique development hota hai. Pakistani context mein, sahi care, regular antenatal visits, aur awareness essential hai healthy pregnancy ke liye.

Pregnancy Trimesters Overview
1st TrimesterWeek 1-12 (3 mahine)
2nd TrimesterWeek 13-26 (3 mahine)
3rd TrimesterWeek 27-40 (3+ mahine)
Full term37-42 weeks
Pre-termBefore 37 weeks
Post-termAfter 42 weeks

FIRST TRIMESTER (Week 1-12)

Week 1-4 — Conception aur Implantation

  • Last menstrual period (LMP) se count shuru
  • Week 2-3 mein conception
  • Week 4 mein implantation
  • Pregnancy test positive (HCG hormone)
  • Symptoms aksar nahi

Week 5-8 — Embryonic Development

Maa ki symptoms:

  • Periods miss
  • Morning sickness shuru (aksar week 6-8)
  • Bohat thakawat
  • Breast tenderness
  • Bar bar pesab
  • Mood swings
  • Smell sensitivity
  • Food cravings ya aversions

Baby ka development:

  • Heart beat shuru hota hai (week 5-6)
  • Brain, spine, organs develop
  • Limb buds appear
  • Size: pea (week 6) → grape (week 8)

Week 9-12 — Fetal Stage Begins

Maa ki changes:

  • Morning sickness peak phir kam ho jati
  • Energy slowly wapas
  • Pet thoda visible (slim women mein)
  • First trimester ultrasound (NT scan)

Baby:

  • Sab major organs ban gaye
  • Fingers aur toes alag
  • Movements (maa feel nahi karti abhi)
  • Size: lemon (12 weeks)

1st Trimester Mein Karne Wale Kaam:

  • Gynecologist se confirm (urine test + ultrasound)
  • Folic acid 400 mcg/day shuru karein (neural tube defects prevention)
  • Iron, calcium supplements
  • Initial blood tests: CBC, blood group, HBsAg, HIV, syphilis, thyroid, blood sugar
  • NT scan (week 11-13) — Down's syndrome screening
  • Smoking, alcohol band
  • Caffeine kam (200 mg/day max)
  • Raw food avoid (sushi, raw eggs)
  • Cat litter avoid (toxoplasmosis)

Common Concerns:

  • Morning sickness: Ginger, small frequent meals, B6
  • Constipation: Fiber, water, walking
  • Acidity: Antacids (doctor mashware se)
  • Bleeding: Light spotting OK, heavy/red — foran doctor

SECOND TRIMESTER (Week 13-26)

Week 13-16 — Honeymoon Phase Begins

Maa ki changes:

  • Morning sickness mostly gone
  • Energy back
  • Pet visible
  • Maternity clothes ki zarurat
  • Skin glow
  • Constipation, indigestion may continue

Baby:

  • Hair, eyebrows develop
  • External genitalia formed (gender determination ultrasound)
  • Bones harden
  • Size: avocado (16 weeks)

Week 17-20 — Quickening (Baby Ki Pehli Movement)

  • First baby movement (quickening) feel hoti hai
  • Initially flutters jaisa
  • Anomaly scan / Level 2 ultrasound (week 18-20) — important!
  • Gender confirmed
  • Round ligament pain (lower belly)

Week 21-26 — Significant Growth

Maa:

  • Belly significantly bigger
  • Stretch marks shuru ho sakte
  • Backache
  • Leg cramps (calcium deficiency)
  • Glucose challenge test (week 24-28) — gestational diabetes
  • Tdap vaccine (week 27-36)

Baby:

  • Lungs developing
  • Eyes open
  • Hearing develops — voice recognize
  • Sleep-wake cycles
  • Size: papaya (24 weeks)
  • Viability outside uterus (24+ weeks with intensive care)

2nd Trimester Mein Karne Wale Kaam:

  • Anomaly scan (week 18-22) — most important ultrasound
  • Glucose tolerance test (24-28 weeks)
  • Iron supplements continue
  • Antenatal classes shuru
  • Maternity belt backache ke liye
  • Sleep on left side (better blood flow)
  • Calcium intake (1000 mg/day)

Pregnancy ke kisi bhi stage mein gynecologist se mashwara? Ilaaj AI pe female doctors available 24/7 — ghar baithe, private.

Gynecologist Se Mashwara

THIRD TRIMESTER (Week 27-40)

Week 27-30 — Big Growth Phase

Maa:

  • Significant fatigue wapas
  • Heartburn worsening
  • Sans phoolna (uterus presses diaphragm)
  • Bar bar pesab
  • Trouble sleeping
  • Stretch marks
  • Hath payon mein soojan
  • Carpal tunnel syndrome possible
  • Braxton Hicks contractions (false labor)

Baby:

  • Brain rapidly developing
  • Eyes open and close
  • Fat layer developing
  • Size: cauliflower (28 weeks)

Week 31-34 — Final Preparations

  • Baby moves to head-down position (most cases)
  • Lung surfactant developing
  • Bones almost fully developed (skull soft for delivery)
  • Hospital bag pack
  • Birth plan finalize
  • Pediatrician select

Week 35-37 — Almost There

Maa:

  • Pelvic pressure
  • Lightening (baby drops lower)
  • Cervix may start dilating
  • Mucus plug discharge
  • Bowels active
  • "Nesting" instinct

Baby:

  • Termed "early term"
  • Brain growing rapidly
  • Fat continues
  • Position fixed

Week 38-40 — Full Term

  • Baby fully developed
  • Average weight 3-3.5 kg
  • Average length 50 cm
  • Labor signs:
    • Regular contractions (every 5 min, 1 minute long, 1 hour)
    • Water breaking
    • Bloody show
    • Severe back pain

3rd Trimester Mein Karne Wale Kaam:

  • Bi-weekly visits 28-36 weeks, weekly thereafter
  • Group B Strep test (week 35-37)
  • Hospital tour
  • Hospital bag pack
  • Birth plan finalize
  • Breastfeeding classes
  • Pediatrician selection
  • Newborn essentials (clothes, diapers, crib)

Warning Signs — Foran Doctor

Any Trimester:

  • Severe abdominal pain
  • Vaginal bleeding (heavy)
  • Severe headache
  • Blurred vision
  • Severe vomiting (cannot keep fluids)
  • High fever
  • Burning urination
  • Sudden severe swelling
  • No fetal movement (after 20 weeks)

Pre-eclampsia (Serious):

  • BP 140/90+
  • Severe headache
  • Vision changes
  • Sudden swelling (face, hands)
  • Right upper abdominal pain
  • Sudden weight gain

Preterm Labor (Before 37 Weeks):

  • Regular contractions (every 10 min)
  • Pelvic pressure
  • Lower back pain
  • Vaginal discharge change
  • Water breaking

Diet Aur Lifestyle

Eat:

  • Whole grains (atta roti, brown rice, oats)
  • Protein: anday, machli (low-mercury), chicken, daal
  • Calcium: doodh, dahi, palak, broccoli
  • Iron: palak, gosht (cooked well), khajoor
  • Folate: green leafy vegetables
  • Vitamin C: kinno, mosmi, capsicum
  • Healthy fats: badam, akhrot

Avoid:

  • Raw meat, sushi, raw eggs
  • Unpasteurized dairy
  • High-mercury fish (king mackerel, swordfish)
  • Excess caffeine (200 mg/day max)
  • Alcohol (zero tolerance)
  • Smoking, paan, gutka
  • Soft cheeses (Brie, Camembert)
  • Liver (high vitamin A)
  • Some herbal teas

Pakistani Context

Cultural Considerations:

  • Family pressure for gender — gender selection unethical aur illegal
  • Joint family support helpful but advice may conflict with medical guidance
  • Traditional practices (post-natal heating, certain foods) — discuss with gynecologist
  • Religious considerations: Ramadan rozay (doctor consultation zaruri)
  • Hospital choice: Government, private, charity (Edhi, Bait-ul-Maal)

Common Issues in Pakistan:

  • Anemia (iron deficiency) — 50%+ pregnant women
  • Vitamin D deficiency
  • Calcium deficiency
  • Gestational diabetes (South Asian higher risk)
  • Pre-eclampsia

Postpartum (Quick Mention)

Delivery ke baad:

  • Postpartum bleeding (lochia) — 4-6 weeks
  • Breastfeeding establishment
  • Sleep deprivation
  • Postpartum depression possible (Pakistan mein under-recognized)
  • Family planning consultation 6 weeks postpartum
  • Doctor follow-up 6 weeks

Conclusion

Pregnancy ek amazing journey hai jo proper care, regular antenatal visits, healthy diet, aur awareness se safer aur joyful banti hai. Pakistan mein hamla khwateen ko quality healthcare ki zarurat hai — clinic visits, lab tests, ultrasounds, aur emergency support. Ilaaj AI pe female gynecologists 24/7 available hain — pregnancy concerns, regular check-ins, aur emergency guidance, ghar baithe Roman Urdu mein.

Frequently Asked Questions

Pregnancy ki shuru ki alamat kya hain?

Common early signs (week 4-8): periods miss, morning sickness (mitli/ulti — aksar week 6-8), bohat thakawat, breast tenderness, bar bar pesab, mood swings, smell sensitivity, food cravings ya aversions. Pregnancy test (urine, week 4+) confirm karta hai. Doctor confirmation aur first ultrasound week 6-8 mein. Symptoms har woman mein different ho sakte.

Pregnancy mein kab gynecologist se milein?

Pregnancy confirm hone pe foran first appointment. Then schedule: 1st trimester — every 4 weeks, 2nd trimester — every 4 weeks, 3rd trimester week 28-36 — every 2 weeks, week 36+ — weekly. Important scans: NT scan (11-13 weeks), anomaly scan (18-22 weeks), glucose tolerance test (24-28 weeks). High-risk pregnancies mein zyada frequent visits.

Pregnancy mein kya khaana chahiye aur kya avoid?

Eat: whole grains (atta roti, brown rice), protein (anday, machli low-mercury, chicken, daal), calcium (doodh, palak), iron (palak, well-cooked gosht, khajoor), folate (green leafy vegetables), vitamin C fruits, healthy fats (badam, akhrot). Avoid: raw meat/eggs/sushi, unpasteurized dairy, high-mercury fish, excess caffeine (max 200 mg/day), alcohol completely, smoking/paan, soft cheeses, liver, some herbal teas.

Pregnancy mein kaunsi medications safe hain?

Generally safe: Paracetamol (Panadol — fever/pain), iron supplements, calcium, folic acid, prenatal vitamins, Lactulose (constipation), ranitidine/Risek (acidity — doctor mashware se), penicillins (Augmentin) and cephalosporins (Velosef) for infections. Avoid completely: Brufen/Voltral/Ponstan (3rd trimester especially), Aspirin (high dose), Disprin, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, isotretinoin (Roaccutane). Hamesha doctor consultation zaruri.

Pregnancy mein warning signs kab doctor ko foran milein?

Foran doctor/ER agar: severe abdominal pain, heavy vaginal bleeding, severe headache, blurred vision, severe vomiting (fluids nahi rok sakte), high fever, burning urination, sudden severe swelling (face/hands), no fetal movement (20+ weeks), BP 140/90+ (pre-eclampsia warning), regular contractions before 37 weeks (preterm labor), water breaking before 37 weeks. Yeh sab serious complications ki nishani ho sakti hain.
